tasks.title: Define the typed navigation config model
tasks.description: Create the TypeScript types for the single account-navigation config: NavItem (id, i18nKey, href, icon, requiredPermission?, surfaces[], tourTarget?), NavGroup (id, i18nKey label, items[], visibility rule), NavSurface enum (sidebar | avatar | mobile), and DiscoveryDoor (id, i18nKey, kind: 'listing'|'partner', href, options[]). Tests: unit test in apps/web/test that the types compile and a sample config object is assignable; no runtime logic yet.

tasks.title: Scaffold the nav config module + per-surface selector
tasks.description: Create the config module (location per OQ-2 — follow existing apps/web config conventions, e.g. src/config/ or src/lib/navigation/) exporting an empty typed config plus a pure selector getNavForSurface({ surface, permissions }) returning the subset for that surface. Tests: unit tests for the selector shape (empty config → empty result) in apps/web/test.

subtasks.title: Config module skeleton at agreed location (OQ-2)

tasks.title: Populate the config with current groups, items and permissions
tasks.description: Model the full baseline inventory into the new IA: Mi cuenta + Turista (always); Anfitrión (dense, absorbs 'Mis alojamientos' moved out of the old activity group); Aliados cabinet (light B2B, header suppressed when single item). Each item carries its requiredPermission. Reference AccountLayout.astro navGroups + account-roles.ts for the source list. Tests: unit tests asserting group membership (Mis alojamientos ∈ Anfitrión, not Turista) and that every item has a permission or is public.

subtasks.title: Anfitrión group with Mis alojamientos moved in

tasks.title: Add i18n keys and migrate hardcoded labels
tasks.description: Add es/en/pt keys for the new groups (Turista, Anfitrión, Aliados/Partners/Parceiros) and doors (Publicá en Hospeda / List on Hospeda / Publique no Hospeda; Sumate como aliado / Become a partner / Seja parceiro; plus the stateful 'Sumá otra alianza'). Migrate the hardcoded TEXTS/AUTH_TEXTS in UserMenu.client.tsx and MobileMenu.client.tsx to t(). Tests: pnpm check-locales passes with no missing keys.

subtasks.title: Migrate UserMenu hardcoded labels to t()
Migrate MobileMenu hardcoded labels to t()

tasks.title: Implement unified permission-based gating
tasks.description: Add a helper that resolves item/group visibility from PermissionEnum (never role checks). Replace isHostRole/isCommerceOwnerRole usage for menu gating; admin-panel entry gated by access.panelAdmin (covers admin/super_admin/editor). Tests: unit tests — a user with accommodation permission sees Anfitrión; without it does not; access.panelAdmin toggles the admin link.

tasks.title: Implement door-state logic (acquired vs missing verticals)
tasks.description: ⚠ Depends on OQ-3 (per-user signal source — permissions vs entitlements vs active-verticals query). Resolve OQ-3 with owner before starting. Implement a resolver that, given the user, returns which listing verticals and which partner roles are acquired vs available, driving door visibility (door lives while ≥1 option missing) and the stateful partner-door label. Tests: unit tests for door visibility transitions and label switch (0 → 'Sumate como aliado', ≥1 → 'Sumá otra alianza', all → hidden).

tasks.title: Refactor the sidebar to consume the config
tasks.description: Rewrite AccountLayout.astro navGroups to render from the single config via getNavForSurface('sidebar'), applying the new role IA (Anfitrión group with Mis alojamientos, Aliados cabinet, no phantom groups). Preserve active-state matching and data-tour targets. Tests: existing AccountLayout/sidebar tests updated + assertions that host sees Anfitrión, tourist does not.

tasks.title: Refactor UserMenu to curated quick-access
tasks.description: Rewrite UserMenu.client.tsx to render the curated subset via getNavForSurface('avatar'): identity + 'Mi cuenta', quick shortcuts (Favoritos + active-role-panel shortcut + Suscripción), session (lang/theme/signout/admin-panel). Remove hardcoded TEXTS. Tests: component tests that non-curated items are absent and the active-role shortcut appears only with the role.

tasks.title: Refactor MobileMenu to single accordion surface
tasks.description: Rewrite MobileMenu.client.tsx to render the full account taxonomy from getNavForSurface('mobile') as collapsed accordions in the agreed order (site nav → account block → session), replacing the hardcoded 4-link list. Logged-out variant: site nav + auth + lang/theme. Tests: component tests for accordion structure and logged-out variant.

tasks.title: Build the generic discovery hub page component
tasks.description: Create a reusable internal hub component under /mi-cuenta that lists a door's options, each with a mini-explanation and a button; acquired options render with a check + 'Gestionar' (links to the managing group) instead of the acquire CTA, driven by the T-006 resolver. Tests: component tests for acquired vs available rendering.

tasks.title: Wire the two doors, routes and stateful text
tasks.description: ⚠ Touches OQ-1 (hub routes/layout — confirm with owner). Instantiate the two hubs ('Publicá en Hospeda' listings, 'Sumate como aliado' partners) on internal /mi-cuenta routes, wire the door entries in sidebar + mobile to them, and apply the stateful partner-door label ('Sumate como aliado' → 'Sumá otra alianza'). Tests: integration test that a door hides once all its options are acquired.

tasks.title: Unit tests — config selectors and permission gating
tasks.description: Cross-cutting unit coverage: getNavForSurface returns correct subset per surface; permission gating shows/hides groups (tourist vs host vs commerce vs staff); no phantom groups. Files: apps/web/test/navigation/*. Run the modified files' existing tests too, not only new ones.

tasks.title: Tests — door state and i18n completeness
tasks.description: Test door visibility transitions, the stateful partner-door label switch, and hub acquired/available rendering; run pnpm check-locales for full es/en/pt coverage of the new keys. Files: apps/web/test/navigation/doors.test.ts.

tasks.title: Close-out — AC verification and notes
tasks.description: Verify AC-1..AC-10 from the spec against the implementation, run pnpm typecheck + lint + test green, and fill closeout.md. No new code.
